scope.shape: pure-standard (meta + entries/messages/list sections only; no for-loops, no h3, no local helpers)

necessityProof.blockerResolved: Five managed-audit / fake-transport report renderers still hand-built the same title/meta/section Markdown frame.
necessityProof.laterConsumer: N1 final acceptance can now classify these gate/decision/echo/intake reports as builder-backed.
necessityProof.reuseDecision: All five are pure-standard shape, so the shared builder fully owns their output; no local lines sections were needed (unlike batch 22).
necessityProof.growthStopCondition: No new helper added. A shared nested-section formatter is still deferred until the controlled-shard-preview h3 grammar proves repetitive.

notes.dynamicTitle: JavaMiniKvRuntimeExecutionCanonicalApprovalInputPrecheckIntake uses a dynamic title (profile.title), mapped as title: profile.title (not a literal).

focusedRendererBatch.command: npx vitest run <5 renderer tests> test/governanceGrowthRatchet.test.ts

byteIdentical.method: Proven by the five renderers' existing tests, which assert the full Markdown output and were not modified (playbook hard rule 1). No temporary compare script was needed this batch.

lint.note: 0 errors; existing warnings are tracked as N5 lint debt and were not introduced by the v2145 documentation-only closeout.
